info.description:
AI-powered Cloud Architecture Translator to Azure.

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key passed via the `X-API-Key` header.
Generate keys at `POST /api/keys`.

## Webhooks

Subscribe to real-time events (analysis completed, IaC generated, etc.) via `POST /api/webhooks`. Deliveries include HMAC-SHA256 signatures in the `X-Archmorph-Signature` header.

## Integrations

Push analysis results to Slack, Microsoft Teams, Jira, and GitHub via the `/api/integrations/*` endpoints.

## Rate Limiting

Default: 200 requests/minute per IP. Per-key limits are configurable at key creation time.

get.description: List all agents (excludes archived).

schema.title: Response List Agents Api Agent Paas Agents Get

post.description: Create a new agent with name, model, and system prompt.

get.description: Get a single agent by ID.

post.description:
Execute an agent with a user message using a ReAct loop.

1. Load agent config (system prompt, tools, RAG collections)
2. If RAG collections configured, search for relevant context
3. Build messages array: system prompt + RAG context + user message
4. Call OpenAI with function calling (if tools attached)
5. ReAct loop (max 3 iterations): tool calls → observe → re-call
6. Track token usage and calculate cost

post.description: Kicks off an async Terraform deployment and streams the logs back to the client.
Runs security and cost estimations before deployment.

POST /api/deployments/execute
Trigger the main deployment (az deployment create or terraform apply).

POST /api/deployments/preview
Dry-run preview (what-if for Bicep or terraform plan).

POST /api/deployments/{job_id}/rollback
Trigger an automated rollback (state revert or terraform destroy / resource group cleanup).

get.description:
GET /api/deployments/{job_id}/status
Check the current status of the requested deployment job.

GET /api/deployments/{job_id}/stream
Stream logs for an ongoing deployment operation.

post.description:
Add Azure services to an architecture using natural language.

Example: "Add a Redis cache and API Gateway with WAF"

The services are added to the existing analysis, and users can continue
to the guided questions or IaC generation.

Analyze an uploaded architecture diagram using GPT-4o vision.

Detects cloud services and maps them to Azure equivalents using the catalog.
Includes an image classification pre-check to reject non-architecture images.

Start an async analysis of an uploaded diagram.

Returns ``202 Accepted`` with a ``job_id``. Use the SSE stream
endpoint ``GET /api/jobs/{job_id}/stream`` to receive real-time
progress events, or poll ``GET /api/jobs/{job_id}`` for status.
Apply user answers to refine the Azure architecture analysis.

get.description: Analyze architecture against Azure Well-Architected Framework.

Map detected Azure services to compliance framework controls.

Returns per-framework readiness scores, covered controls, gaps,
and prioritised remediation recommendations.

parameters.description: Comma-separated framework IDs: soc2, hipaa, pci_dss, gdpr, iso27001, fedramp

schema.title: Frameworks

get.description: Export compliance report as JSON or Markdown.
Return gap analysis sorted by severity across requested frameworks.

Return enriched cost data for the Pricing tab.

Includes per-service formula, assumptions, alternative SKUs,
optimization recommendations, cost-by-category, and source vs
target comparison.
Estimate monthly Azure costs for the analysed architecture.

post.description: Update per-service cost configuration (instance count, SKU, reserved capacity).

get.description: Get cost estimate with user-configured overrides applied.
Export cost breakdown as CSV with overrides applied.
Show Reserved Instance savings vs pay-as-you-go pricing.
Get cost optimization recommendations for the architecture.
Build a dependency graph from an existing analysis.
Compare two version snapshots and return a structured diff.

post.description:
Generate the customer-facing Architecture Package.

format=html returns the full tabbed package. format=svg returns a single
selected topology SVG where diagram=primary|dr.

Generate an architecture diagram in Excalidraw, Draw.io, Visio, or
Landing-Zone-SVG format.

Set multi_page=true for presentation-ready 4-page exports (Draw.io only, #479).
Set format=landing-zone-svg + dr_variant=primary|dr for the region-aware
landing-zone diagram (#571).

Note (#576): the source provider for the landing-zone diagram is read
implicitly from ``analysis["source_provider"]`` (allowed: "aws"|"gcp",
default "aws"). It is intentionally NOT exposed as a query param so the
frontend stays untouched and the analyzer pipeline remains the single
source of truth. Unknown values raise ``ValueError`` → HTTP 400.

Export HLD document to Word, PDF, or PowerPoint format.

Query params:
  - format: docx | pdf | pptx (required)
  - include_diagrams: true | false (default: true)
  - export_mode: customer | internal (default: internal)
    When 'customer', both architecture diagram and application flow
    diagram MUST be present in the export. If missing, they are
    auto-generated from analysis data. (#357)

Body (optional JSON):
  - diagram_image: base64-encoded architecture diagram image to embed
  - app_flow_image: base64-encoded application flow diagram to embed

Export a complete migration package as a ZIP file.

Includes: IaC code, HLD document (DOCX), cost estimate (JSON), and
analysis summary (JSON). This is the end-to-end migration wizard output.

Body (optional): { "iac_format": "terraform", "include_diagrams": true }

Generate Infrastructure as Code from the architecture analysis.

``force=true`` overrides the architecture-blocker gate (Issue #610).

Start async IaC code generation. Returns 202 with job_id.

``force=true`` overrides the architecture-blocker gate (Issue #610).
Generate a comprehensive High-Level Design document.
Start async HLD document generation. Returns 202 with job_id.

post.description:
Generate Azure network topology translation from a diagram's analysis.

Reads the analysis result from the session store, extracts networking
constructs (VPC, subnets, security groups, route tables), and produces
an Azure-equivalent VNet plan with NSGs, route tables, and NAT gateway.
Send a session-ready notification email to the user.

Query params:

post.description:
Re-inject a cached analysis result into the session store.

The frontend caches analysis data in sessionStorage.  When the backend
restarts and the in-memory store is wiped, the frontend can push its
cached copy here to transparently restore the session.

get.description:
Compute the Migration Risk Score (MRS) for a diagram analysis.

Returns a composite score (0-100) with per-factor breakdown,
risk tier, and actionable recommendations.

Uses simulation mode only — never executes real Terraform CLI

get.description:
Stream real-time progress events via Server-Sent Events.

Event types:
  - ``status``   — Initial job state
  - ``progress`` — Progress update (0-100 + message)
  - ``complete`` — Job finished with result
  - ``error``    — Job failed with error message
  - ``cancelled`` — Job was cancelled

Heartbeat comments (``: heartbeat``) are sent every 5s.
List all active (non-revoked) API keys. Full key values are never returned.

get.description: Get cost estimate with user-configured overrides applied.
Export cost breakdown as CSV with overrides applied.
Show Reserved Instance savings vs pay-as-you-go pricing.

post.description:
Generate the customer-facing Architecture Package.

format=html returns the full tabbed package. format=svg returns a single
selected topology SVG where diagram=primary|dr.

Generate an architecture diagram in Excalidraw, Draw.io, Visio, or
Landing-Zone-SVG format.

Set multi_page=true for presentation-ready 4-page exports (Draw.io only, #479).
Set format=landing-zone-svg + dr_variant=primary|dr for the region-aware
landing-zone diagram (#571).

Note (#576): the source provider for the landing-zone diagram is read
implicitly from ``analysis["source_provider"]`` (allowed: "aws"|"gcp",
default "aws"). It is intentionally NOT exposed as a query param so the
frontend stays untouched and the analyzer pipeline remains the single
source of truth. Unknown values raise ``ValueError`` → HTTP 400.

Export HLD document to Word, PDF, or PowerPoint format.

Query params:
  - format: docx | pdf | pptx (required)
  - include_diagrams: true | false (default: true)
  - export_mode: customer | internal (default: internal)
    When 'customer', both architecture diagram and application flow
    diagram MUST be present in the export. If missing, they are
    auto-generated from analysis data. (#357)

Body (optional JSON):
  - diagram_image: base64-encoded architecture diagram image to embed
  - app_flow_image: base64-encoded application flow diagram to embed

Export a complete migration package as a ZIP file.

Includes: IaC code, HLD document (DOCX), cost estimate (JSON), and
analysis summary (JSON). This is the end-to-end migration wizard output.

Body (optional): { "iac_format": "terraform", "include_diagrams": true }

Generate Infrastructure as Code from the architecture analysis.

``force=true`` overrides the architecture-blocker gate (Issue #610).

Start async IaC code generation. Returns 202 with job_id.

``force=true`` overrides the architecture-blocker gate (Issue #610).
Generate a comprehensive High-Level Design document.
Start async HLD document generation. Returns 202 with job_id.

post.description:
Re-inject a cached analysis result into the session store.

The frontend caches analysis data in sessionStorage.  When the backend
restarts and the in-memory store is wiped, the frontend can push its
cached copy here to transparently restore the session.

risk tier, and actionable recommendations.

get.description:
Stream real-time progress events via Server-Sent Events.

Event types:
  - ``status``   — Initial job state
  - ``progress`` — Progress update (0-100 + message)
  - ``complete`` — Job finished with result
  - ``error``    — Job failed with error message
  - ``cancelled`` — Job was cancelled

Heartbeat comments (``: heartbeat``) are sent every 5s.
